Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $317,101,119Details
International Box Office $469,534,294
Worldwide Box Office $786,635,413
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $119,140,439
Total $119,140,439
Further financial details...

Synopsis

It’s the height of the Cold War, and famous archaeologist Indiana Jones, returning from his latest adventure, finds out his job at Marshall College is in jeopardy. He meets Mutt, a young man who wants Indy to help him find the legendary Crystal Skull of Akator, and the pair set out for Peru. However, deadly agent Irina Spalko is searching for the powerful artifact, too, because the Soviets believe it can help them conquer the world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$100,137,835 (31.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.17 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$185,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,260 opening theaters/4,264 max. theaters, 7.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $528,207,432

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 22nd, 2008 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: May 23rd, 2008 (Wide), released as Indiana Jones And The Kingdom Of The Crystal Skull (United Kingdom)
Video Release: October 14th, 2008 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for adventure violence and scary images.
Running Time: 123 minutes
Franchise: Indiana Jones
Cast, crew, or production detail: Character's Journey Shown on Map, Legacy Sequel, Sequels With Returning Stars
Plot point: Archaeology, Dead Language, Double Agent, Dysfunctional Family, Friendly Alien on Earth, Mental Illness, Psychics, Surprise Twist, Treasure Hunters
Social setting: Cold War
Subgenre: Chase Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Lucasfilm
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, German, Russian

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

It’s the height of the Cold War, and famous archaeologist Indiana Jones, returning from his latest adventure, finds out his job at Marshall College is in jeopardy. He meets Mutt, a young man who wants Indy to help him find the legendary Crystal Skull of Akator, and the pair set out for Peru. However, deadly agent Irina Spalko is searching for the powerful artifact, too, because the Soviets believe it can help them conquer the world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$100,137,835 (31.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.17 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$185,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,260 opening theaters/4,264 max. theaters, 7.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $528,207,432

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 22nd, 2008 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: May 23rd, 2008 (Wide), released as Indiana Jones And The Kingdom Of The Crystal Skull (United Kingdom)
Video Release: October 14th, 2008 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for adventure violence and scary images.
Running Time: 123 minutes
Franchise: Indiana Jones
Cast, crew, or production detail: Character's Journey Shown on Map, Legacy Sequel, Sequels With Returning Stars
